codeReview.summary: The commit adds two same-workspace denial regressions, but they do not actually exercise the documented restart/continue contract boundary. Both new tests call `UpdateTimeEntry` with `Stop=nil`, even though the mission architecture says continue creates a new running entry from a historical entry and the worker transcript itself found `UpdateTimeEntry` preserves an existing stop when `Stop=nil`. As written, the tests prove only generic cross-user update denial on a nearby internal API, not the real restart/continue mutation path the feature was supposed to close.

issues.description: `TestSameWorkspaceUserACannotRestartUserBTimeEntry` models restart by calling `UpdateTimeEntry` with `Stop=nil`, but `UpdateTimeEntry` preserves the existing stop when `Stop` is nil (`apps/backend/internal/tracking/application/service.go:453-459`). The mission guidance also states `UpdateTimeEntry` is not a stopped-entry-to-running conversion path. This test never exercises a real restart surface, so it cannot prove the documented restart mutation is denied.

`TestSameWorkspaceUserACannotContinueUserBTimeEntry` also uses `UpdateTimeEntry` with a new start and `Stop=nil` as a stand-in for continue, but the mission architecture says `Continue Time Entry` creates a new running entry from a historical entry rather than mutating the original row. The worker transcript already discovered there was no documented restart/continue boundary at `UpdateTimeEntry`, yet the implementation still asserted this surrogate path. The feature therefore does not satisfy its requirement to target the real documented restart/continue mutation path.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `backend-test-worker` skill says to read repo `.factory/library/architecture.md` before editing and to stop/return to orchestrator if the requested regression mentions a specific surface like continue/restart but no real documented/API boundary exists. In this session the worker transcript found that `UpdateTimeEntry` was only a nearby internal API and even noted it preserves stop when `Stop=nil`, but still proceeded and reported `followedProcedure: true` instead of treating the missing boundary as a blocker.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill file `.factory/skills/backend-test-worker/SKILL.md` steps 1 and 10; transcript skeleton for session `6360040b-d986-4b9f-a1ca-cc398f3a07ee` shows no read of `.factory/library/architecture.md` and explicitly states `UpdateTimeEntry with command.Stop == nil preserves the existing stop (doesn't restart)` before adding the surrogate tests.

summary: Fail: the fix adds new denial tests, but they target `UpdateTimeEntry` rather than a real restart/continue contract boundary. Because that API does not perform the documented restart/continue behavior, the feature still does not prove the remaining write surface is secured.
